audit_items.proof_trace: All functions in utf-8.c follow the standard C library convention of not checking pointer parameters for NULL before dereference (e.g., ldap_utf8_bytes L50, ldap_utf8_chars L62, ldap_utf8_charlen L90, all ldap_utf8_is* functions). Callers maintain the LDAP berval invariant (bv_len > 0 implies bv_val != NULL): the tls2.c:1634 path handles bv_len==0 safely (loop condition fails), the smbk5pwd.c:471 path is guarded by passwd.c:200-204 which ensures bv_len > 0 before overlay dispatch, and getdn.c:2408 returns early on bv_len==0. The only heap allocation in utf-8.c (ldap_ucs_to_utf8s L270) has a proper NULL check. No concrete path from external LDAP input to a NULL dereference in utf-8.c was found.
